01 / About
WHO I AM

I'm a Mechanical Engineering student at the University of Illinois Chicago (graduating May 2027), passionate about turning complex engineering challenges into precise, manufacturable solutions.

My work spans thermal systems, CAD modeling, FEA simulation, and fabrication — with hands-on research experience at UIC's Intelligent Multiphase Systems (Suh) Lab, where I design and build flow-boiling test modules and implement DAQ systems.

Outside the lab, I co-lead UIC ASME's drivable couch project and contribute to Formula SAE's powertrain subteam — because the best engineers build things, not just simulate them.

02 / Projects
FEATURED WORK
01 Formula SAE · Powertrain
AIR INTAKE REDESIGN
Tools: SolidWorks · Ansys · Machine Shop

Redesigned the air-intake neck with a 4-member team, increasing cross-section by 20% to stabilize airflow and improve engine performance. Optimized plenum-runner-throttle interfaces and built a push-bar lift system — cutting fasteners by 40% and assembly time by 70%.

→
02 Research · Thermal Systems
FLOW-BOILING TEST MODULE
Tools: SolidWorks · LabVIEW · NI DAQ · Machine Shop

Designed 5+ components for a modular flow-boiling test module and closed-loop system at Suh Lab. Developed a closed-loop schematic with pre-heater, flow meter, thermocouples, and pressure sensors — improving repeatability by 25% and data accuracy by 20%.

→
03 ASME · Go-Kart
CUSTOM GO-KART CHASSIS
Tools: SolidWorks · Welding · 3D Printing

Integrated a 9+ HP Tillotson 212 EC engine into a custom SolidWorks-designed chassis, improving stiffness and reducing sourcing time by 25%. Co-developed competition rules using karting standards, reducing inspection time by 30%.

→
04 ASME · Drivable Couch
DRIVABLE COUCH STEERING
Tools: SolidWorks · Custom Fabrication

Co-led a 5+ member steering team for UIC ASME's custom drivable couch build. Selected and sourced the correct steering axle and hardware, ensuring parts met design, safety, and manufacturability requirements. Organized fundraisers averaging $800+ profit each.

→
05 ME-347 · Product Design
FIDGET KEYBOARD CUBE
Tools: SolidWorks · FDM 3D Printing · MakerSpace

Designed a pocket-sized 5-key mini keyboard fidget toy that folds into a cube — fully 3D-printable with no post-assembly. Modeled custom spring mechanisms mimicking key switches and integrated 8 print-in-place hinges for smooth, reliable motion.

→
